content.problemStatement:
A famous student of AESC MSU, as you know, comes from Kostomuksha. Kostomuksha has a popular game called Doka.

The essence of Doka is as follows:

You are given an array AAA and an integer XXX. You want to calculate how many subarrays of this array have a geometric mean of XXX.

Formally, calculate the number of pairs of integers (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N and
AL‚čÖAL+1‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖARR‚ąíL+1=X\sqrt[R-L+1]{A_L \cdot A_{L + 1} \cdot \ldots \cdot A_{R}} = XR‚ąíL+1AL‚Äč‚čÖAL+1‚Äč‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖAR‚Äč‚Äč=X


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,XN, XN,X ‚ÄĒ the size of the array AAA and the required geometric mean.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, on a new line print the number of subarrays that satisfy the condition.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Every subarray has a geometric mean of 333, and there are 666 subarrays in total so the answer is 666.
Test case 222: The only subarray with a geometric mean of 444 is the singleton array [4][4][4], obtained with L=R=4L = R = 4L=R=4.
Test case 333: The 666 pairs of (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) which have a geometric mean of 545454 are:

(1,2)(1, 2)(1,2), giving the subarray [36,81][36, 81][36,81]
(1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), giving the subarray [36,81,54][36, 81, 54][36,81,54]
(1,4)(1, 4)(1,4), giving the subarray [36,81,54,54][36, 81, 54, 54][36,81,54,54]
(3,3)(3, 3)(3,3), giving the subarray [54][54][54]
(3,4)(3, 4)(3,4), giving the subarray [54,54][54, 54][54,54]
(4,4)(4, 4)(4,4), giving the subarray [54][54][54]
